evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: A structured, quality-focused mindset and genuine interest in standards and data governance topics.
evidence.domain_risk: our reporting platform is one of the key channels we use to bring data to life for the business
evidence.data_team_maturity: Support the ongoing modernization of the BI stack, including evaluating and testing alternative analytics and visualization tools.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, develop, and deploy reports and dashboards powered by our Data Warehouse.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support documentation and governance efforts to improve maintainability and trust in reporting assets
evidence.autonomy_level: Improve consistency and usability of reports through establishing shared standards, templates, and best practices
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Support the ongoing modernization of the BI stack, including evaluating and testing alternative analytics and visualization tools.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The quote frames the work as modernizing an existing BI stack rather than building new infrastructure, supporting the fix_scale classification.
evidence.work_arrangement: Workplace Type: Hybrid (Work from abroad up to 30 calendar days per year)
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD focuses on Power BI, SQL, and BI stack modernization with no mention of AI coding tools or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the text.
evidence.testing_framing: A structured, quality-focused mindset and genuine interest in standards and data governance topics.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Quality and governance are framed as a personal mindset/interest rather than an owned testing responsibility or named testing tool, keeping this at 'responsibility' framing driven by the governance ownership language elsewhere (documentation, standards, templates) rather than a specific QA practice.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Support documentation and governance efforts to improve maintainability and trust in reporting assets.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The emphasis on trust, maintainability, and governance reflects an operational reliability concern for the reporting platform rather than explicit compliance or regulatory framing, consistent with a moderate rating.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple quality, governance, standards, and documentation signals with no velocity language.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Language indicates internal business intelligence and reporting without explicit financial reporting, regulatory compliance, or audit signals, suggesting moderate risk where errors affect decisions but lack immediate regulatory consequences.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Existing Power BI platform with modernization efforts indicates mid-stage team, not greenfield nor large established function.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Mix of technically specific responsibilities (Power BI, SQL, data modeling, Snowflake/Redshift, architecture) alongside generic governance and support language.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role primarily serves the data/analytics platform and its internal consumers through governance, standardization, and platform modernization rather than commercial, product, or finance stakeholders.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role combines strategic ownership of platform governance and standards (establishing) with execution of reporting deliverables (designing, deploying).
